k-NN analysis

Vocabulary-based clustering + isolated-outlier detection over recent primary records

v2/knn
Running k-NN…
How it works: Every primary record in the chosen window is fingerprinted (key + json_fingerprint(data)), TF-IDF vectorised, and compared by cosine similarity. Each line picks its k nearest neighbours; lines whose closest peer scores below anomaly_threshold are flagged as outliers. The remaining lines are grouped into clusters via union-find on the k-NN graph. Each cluster's representative is its densest member (highest mean similarity to its top-k peers). Use a tighter threshold (≥ 0.4) to demand stronger evidence; loosen it (~0.05) on small or vocabulary-disjoint corpora.
Scanning recent primaries…
{% endblock %}
